tool_args.label: NYC DOT Bus Lane Camera Enforcement 2024 Report
tool_args.raw_text: New York City Bus Lane Camera Enforcement 2024 Report covering 2022 and 2023

payload.title: New York City Bus Lane Camera Enforcement
payload.description: State legislatively mandated report covering the DOT stationary camera and MTA ABLE bus lane camera enforcement programs for 2022 and 2023

tool_args.label: NYC Department of Transportation (DOT)
tool_args.raw_text: The New York City Department of Transportation (DOT) committed to ensuring fast and reliable bus travel

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
tool_args.raw_text: DOT has worked closely with the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA), MTA New York City Transit (NYCT), and MTA Bus Company (MTA Bus)

tool_args.raw_text: MTA New York City Transit (NYCT)

tool_args.label: DOT Stationary Bus Lane Camera Program
tool_args.raw_text: fixed location camera system implemented by DOT. Two cameras are mounted above the bus lane.

payload.project_name: DOT Stationary Bus Lane Camera Enforcement Program
payload.description: Fixed-location camera system with two cameras mounted above bus lanes - one for license plate view, one for wider street view. Installed at 188 locations through 2023.

tool_args.label: MTA ABLE (Automated Bus Lane Enforcement) Program
tool_args.raw_text: MTA on-bus mobile cameras, first tested in a NYCT pilot program in 2010-2011 and then fully implemented under the ABLE program in 2019

payload.project_name: MTA ABLE (Automated Bus Lane Enforcement) Program
payload.description: On-bus mobile camera system operated by MTA. Equipment installed inside buses captures rear license plates of vehicles stopped in bus lanes. Two buses must observe same vehicle at same GPS location at least 5 minutes apart to issue violation. Captures no standing and no parking violations.

tool_args.label: MTA ACE (Automated Camera Enforcement) Program
tool_args.raw_text: MTA transitioned from the ABLE program to the Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE) program which issues additional violations for vehicles parking in bus stops and double parking along bus routes

payload.description: Expanded program that transitioned from ABLE, issuing additional violations for vehicles parking in bus stops and double parking along bus routes. Began enforcement after a 60-day warning period in summer 2024.

tool_args.label: Select Bus Service (SBS) Program
tool_args.raw_text: Select Bus Service program integrated camera-enforced bus lanes with a suite of corridor-specific improvements

payload.description: SBS program integrated camera-enforced bus lanes with corridor-specific improvements. Camera enforcement began on SBS corridors in November 2010. Led to bus speed increases of 15% to 31% depending on route.

tool_args.label: Better Buses Program
tool_args.raw_text: DOT initiated the Better Buses program in 2019 to further the goals of SBS on a broader array of bus corridors. In 2022 and 2023, DOT installed 23.1 miles of new and upgraded bus lanes.

payload.description: Program initiated in 2019 to further SBS goals on a broader array of bus corridors. In 2022-2023, installed 23.1 miles of new and upgraded bus lanes.

tool_args.label: SBS speed increases from camera-enforced bus lanes

payload.claim_text: The Select Bus Service program, which integrated camera-enforced bus lanes with a suite of corridor-specific improvements, led to notable bus speed increases ranging from 15% to 31%, depending on the route.

tool_args.label: DOT stationary bus lane camera system
tool_args.raw_text: Two cameras mounted above the bus lane. One camera provides a high-quality view of the rear of a vehicle, clearly showing the vehicle's license plate. The second camera provides a wider-angle view of the street.

payload.description: Fixed-location camera system with two cameras per location: one license plate camera and one wide-angle street view camera. Mounted above bus lanes, often approaching right turn locations. Captures driving in bus lane violations.

tool_args.label: MTA ABLE on-bus mobile camera system
tool_args.raw_text: Equipment installed inside of the bus that can capture the rear license plates of vehicles stopped in the bus lane as the bus passes the vehicle. It then uses GPS to mark that location.

payload.description: On-bus mobile camera system. Equipment inside bus captures rear license plates of stopped vehicles, uses GPS to mark location. Two buses must observe same vehicle at same GPS location at least 5 minutes apart to issue violation. Records no standing and no parking violations.

tool_args.label: Better Buses corridor installations 2022-2023
tool_args.raw_text: University Avenue and Gun Hill Road in the Bronx, 1st Avenue, 3rd Avenue, and Avenues A and D in Manhattan, 21st Street and Northern Boulevard in Queens, and Livingston Street in Brooklyn

payload.description: 23.1 miles of new and upgraded bus lanes installed in 2022 and 2023 under the Better Buses program

tool_args.label: 2010 legislative authority for bus lane cameras
tool_args.raw_text: In the summer of 2010, New York City and the MTA were given legislative authority to begin operating camera-based bus lane enforcement on SBS corridors

payload.description: Legislative authority granted for camera-based bus lane enforcement on SBS corridors in each borough

tool_args.label: First stationary cameras installed November 2010
tool_args.raw_text: DOT installs and maintains stationary, street-mounted bus lane enforcement cameras beginning in November 2010

payload.description: Initiated implementation of stationary bus lane camera enforcement system

tool_args.label: SBS starts on M15 in Manhattan 2010
tool_args.raw_text: NYCT conducted bus and subway media campaigns to raise bus lane awareness, beginning in 2010 with the start of SBS on the M15 in Manhattan

payload.description: Select Bus Service launched on the M15 route in Manhattan, with simultaneous bus lane awareness campaign

tool_args.label: 2019 legislative extension to all bus lanes
tool_args.raw_text: In 2019, State legislative authority was extended to allow the operation of camera-based enforcement on all bus lanes within New York City, not just SBS corridors

payload.description: State legislative authority extended to allow camera-based enforcement on all bus lanes within NYC, not just SBS corridors. Added restrictions on time, fine amounts, and enforcement methods.

tool_args.label: Better Buses program initiated 2019
tool_args.raw_text: DOT initiated the Better Buses program in 2019 to further the goals of SBS on a broader array of bus corridors

payload.description: Better Buses program initiated to further SBS goals on broader array of bus corridors

tool_args.label: ABLE program fully implemented 2019
tool_args.raw_text: ABLE program fully implemented in 2019

payload.description: MTA ABLE (Automated Bus Lane Enforcement) program fully implemented, following 2010-2011 NYCT pilot

tool_args.label: ACE program goes into effect 2024
tool_args.raw_text: In 2024 expanded legislative authority went into effect, and MTA transitioned from the ABLE program to the Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE) program

payload.description: MTA transitioned from ABLE to ACE program, issuing additional violations for vehicles parking in bus stops and double parking along bus routes. Enforcement began after 60-day warning period in summer 2024.

tool_args.label: Missing bus speed, reliability, and ridership data
tool_args.raw_text: expanded legislative authority which went into effect in 2024 included additional reporting requirements on bus speeds, reliability, and ridership. These newer reporting components are not a part of this report, as data was not collected with this purpose in mind in 2022 and 2023.

payload.missing_information: Bus speeds, reliability, and ridership before and after implementation of the bus rapid transit demonstration program for each bus route, including current statistics
payload.description: 2024 legislation added new reporting requirements not yet collected for 2022-2023. Next two-year report will incorporate this data.
